   106→#   $* - message to log
   107→log_warn() {
   108→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   109→    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
   110→        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
   111→        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
   112→    fi
   113→}
   114→
   115→# Log error message
   116→# Args:
   117→#   $* - message to log
   118→log_error() {
   119→    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
   120→    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
   121→}
   122→
   123→# Internal: Write log entry to file
   124→# Args:
   125→#   $1 - log level
   126→#   $2 - message
   127→_log_to_file() {
   128→    local level="$1"
   129→    shift
   130→    local message="$*"
   131→    local timestamp
   132→    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
   133→
   134→    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
   135→        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
   136→    fi
   137→}
   138→
   139→# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
   140→# Args:
   141→#   $1 - log level
   142→#   $2 - message
   143→_log_format_ansi() {
   144→    local level="$1"
   145→    shift
   146→    local message="$*"
   147→    local color=""
   148→
   149→    case "$level" in
   150→        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
   151→        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
   152→        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
   153→        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
   154→        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
   155→    esac
   156→
   157→    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
   158→}
   159→
   160→# ============================================================================
   161→# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
   162→# ============================================================================
   163→
   164→# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
   165→# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
   166→# Globals:
   167→#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
   168→config_load() {
   169→    local sidekick_root
   170→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   171→
   172→    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
   173→    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
   174→    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
   175→        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
   176→        exit 2
   177→    fi
   178→    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   179→    source "$defaults"
   180→
   181→    # 2. Load user config (optional)
   182→    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   183→    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
   184→        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   185→        source "$user_config"
   186→    fi
   187→
   188→    # 3. Load project config (optional)
   189→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   190→        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   191→        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
   192→            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   193→            source "$project_config"
   194→        fi
   195→    fi
   196→
   197→    # Validate configuration
   198→    _config_validate
   199→}
   200→
   201→# Get configuration value
   202→# Args:
   203→#   $1 - config key name
   204→# Returns:
   205→#   Config value or empty string
   206→config_get() {
   207→    local key="$1"
   208→    echo "${!key:-}"
   209→}
   210→
   211→# Check if feature is enabled
   212→# Args:
   213→#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
   214→# Returns:
   215→#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
   216→config_is_feature_enabled() {
   217→    local feature="$1"
   218→    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
   219→    local value="${!key:-false}"
   220→
   221→    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
   222→        return 0
   223→    else
   224→        return 1
   225→    fi
   226→}
   227→
   228→# Internal: Validate configuration values
   229→# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
   230→_config_validate() {
   231→    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
   232→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   233→    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
   234→        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
   235→        LOG_LEVEL="info"
   236→    fi
   237→
   238→    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
   239→    local numeric_vars=(
   240→        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
   241→        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
   242→        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
   243→        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
   244→        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
   245→        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
   246→        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
   247→        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
   248→        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
   249→        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
   250→        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
   251→        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
   252→        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
   253→        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
   254→    )
   255→
   256→    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
   257→        local value="${!var:-0}"
   258→        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
   259→            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
   260→            return 1
   261→        fi
   262→    done
   263→
   264→    return 0
   265→}
   266→
   267→# ============================================================================
   268→# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
   269→# ============================================================================
   270→
   271→# Detect execution scope (user or project)
   272→# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
   273→# Returns:
   274→#   "user" or "project"
   275→path_detect_scope() {
   276→    local script_path
   277→    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")
   278→
   279→    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
   280→        echo "user"
   281→    else
   282→        echo "project"
   283→    fi
   284→}
   285→
   286→# Get sidekick installation directory
   287→# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
   288→# Globals:
   289→#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
   290→path_get_sidekick_root() {
   291→    # Return cached value if available
   292→    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
   293→        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
   294→        return 0
   295→    fi
   296→
   297→    # Determine root from this script's location
   298→    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
   299→    local script_dir
   300→    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
   301→    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)
   302→
   303→    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
   304→}
   305→
   306→# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
   307→# Args:
   308→#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
   309→# Returns:
   310→#   Absolute path to session directory
   311→path_get_session_dir() {
   312→    local session_id="$1"
   313→    local sidekick_root
   314→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   315→    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"
   316→
   317→    # Create if doesn't exist
   318→    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   319→        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
   320→    fi
   321→
   322→    echo "$session_dir"
   323→}
   324→
   325→# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
   326→# Args:
   327→#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
   328→# Returns:
   329→#   Absolute path to project directory
   330→path_get_project_dir() {
   331→    local json="${1:-}"
   332→
   333→    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
   334→    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
   335→        local from_json
   336→        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
   337→        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
   338→            _path_normalize "$from_json"
   339→            return 0
   340→        fi
   341→    fi
   342→
   343→    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
   344→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   345→        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   346→        return 0
   347→    fi
   348→
   349→    # Fall back to current directory
   350→    pwd
   351→}
   352→
   353→# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
   354→# Args:
   355→#   $1 - path to normalize
   356→# Returns:
   357→#   Normalized absolute path
   358→_path_normalize() {
   359→    local path="$1"
   360→
   361→    # Resolve to absolute path
   362→    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
   363→        readlink -f "$path"
   364→    else
   365→        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
   366→        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
   367→    fi
   368→}
   369→
   370→# ============================================================================
   371→# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
   372→# ============================================================================
   373→
   374→# Extract value from JSON using jq
   375→# Args:
   376→#   $1 - JSON string
   377→#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
   378→# Returns:
   379→#   Extracted value or empty string
   380→json_get() {
   381→    local json="$1"
   382→    local path="$2"
   383→
   384→    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
   385→}
   386→
   387→# Extract session_id from JSON
   388→# Args:
   389→#   $1 - JSON string
   390→# Returns:
   391→#   Session ID or empty string
   392→json_get_session_id() {
   393→    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
   394→}
   395→
   396→# Extract transcript_path from JSON
   397→# Args:
   398→#   $1 - JSON string
   399→# Returns:
   400→#   Transcript path or empty string
   401→json_get_transcript_path() {
   402→    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
   403→}
   404→
   405→# Validate JSON syntax
   406→# Args:
   407→#   $1 - JSON string
   408→# Returns:
   409→#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
   410→json_validate() {
   411→    local json="$1"
   412→    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
   413→}
   414→
   415→# Extract JSON from markdown code block
   416→# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
   417→# Args:
   418→#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
   419→# Returns:
   420→#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
   421→json_extract_from_markdown() {
   422→    local text="$1"
   423→
   424→    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
   425→    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
   426→        # Extract content between ```json and ```
   427→        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
   428→    else
   429→        # Return as-is
   430→        echo "$text"
   431→    fi
   432→}
   433→
   434→# ============================================================================
   435→# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
   436→# ============================================================================
   437→
   438→# Launch background process with PID tracking
   439→# Args:
   440→#   $1 - session_id
   441→#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
   442→#   $3 - function name to execute
   443→#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
   444→# Creates:
   445→#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
   446→#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
   447→process_launch_background() {
   448→    local session_id="$1"
   449→    local name="$2"
   450→    local function="$3"
   451→    shift 3
   452→    local args=("$@")
   453→
   454→    local session_dir
   455→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   456→    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
   457→    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"
   458→
   459→    # Check if already running
   460→    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   461→        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
   462→        return 0
   463→    fi
   464→
   465→    # Launch background process
   466→    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"
   467→
   468→    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
   469→    nohup bash -c "
   470→        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
   471→        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'
   472→
   473→        # Call the function with args
   474→        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
   475→    " &
   476→
   477→    local pid=$!
   478→    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
   479→
   480→    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
   481→}
   482→
   483→# Check if process is running by PID file
   484→# Args:
   485→#   $1 - PID file path
   486→# Returns:
   487→#   0 if running, 1 if not running
   488→process_is_running() {
   489→    local pid_file="$1"
   490→
   491→    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   492→        return 1
   493→    fi
   494→
   495→    local pid
   496→    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
   497→
   498→    # Check if PID exists and is running
   499→    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   500→        return 0
   501→    else
   502→        return 1
   503→    fi
   504→}
   505→
   506→# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
   507→# Args:
   508→#   $1 - PID file path
   509→process_kill() {
   510→    local pid_file="$1"
   511→
   512→    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   513→        return 0
   514→    fi
   515→
   516→    local pid
   517→    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
   518→
   519→    # Try graceful shutdown first
   520→    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   521→        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
   522→        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   523→
   524→        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
   525→        local waited=0
   526→        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
   527→            sleep 1
   528→            waited=$((waited + 1))
   529→        done
   530→
   531→        # Force kill if still running
   532→        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   533→            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
   534→            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   535→        fi
   536→    fi
   537→
   538→    # Remove PID file
   539→    rm -f "$pid_file"
   540→}
   541→
   542→# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
   543→# Args:
   544→#   $1 - session directory path
   545→process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
   546→    local session_dir="$1"
   547→
   548→    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   549→        return 0
   550→    fi
   551→
   552→    # Find all .pid files
   553→    local pid_file
   554→    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
   555→        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   556→            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   557→                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
   558→                rm -f "$pid_file"
   559→            fi
   560→        fi
   561→    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
   562→}
   563→
   564→# ============================================================================
   565→# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
   566→# ============================================================================
   567→
   568→# Find Claude CLI binary
   569→# Returns:
   570→#   Path to claude binary
   571→# Exits:
   572→#   3 if claude binary not found
   573→claude_find_bin() {
   574→    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
   575→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
   576→        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
   577→        return 0
   578→    fi
   579→
   580→    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude
   581→    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
   582→    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
   583→        echo "$local_claude"
   584→        return 0
   585→    fi
   586→
   587→    # 3. Check PATH
   588→    if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
   589→        command -v claude
   590→        return 0
   591→    fi
   592→
   593→    # Not found
   594→    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
   595→    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
   596→    exit 3
   597→}
   598→
   599→# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
   600→# Args:
   601→#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
   602→#   $2 - prompt text
   603→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
   604→# Returns:
   605→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   606→# Exits:
   607→#   1 on error
   608→claude_invoke() {
   609→    local model="$1"
   610→    local prompt="$2"
   611→    local timeout="${3:-30}"
   612→
   613→    local claude_bin
   614→    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)
   615→
   616→    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   617→    local workspace
   618→    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")
   619→
   620→    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   621→
   622→    # Invoke Claude with timeout
   623→    local output
   624→    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
   625→        --model "$model" \
   626→        --workspace "$workspace" \
   627→        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
   628→        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
   629→        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   630→        return 1
   631→    fi
   632→
   633→    # Cleanup workspace
   634→    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   635→
   636→    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
   637→    local json
   638→    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")
   639→
   640→    # Validate JSON
   641→    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
   642→        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
   643→        return 1
   644→    fi
   645→
   646→    echo "$json"
   647→}
   648→
   649→# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
   650→# Args:
   651→#   $1 - Claude output text
   652→# Returns:
   653→#   Extracted JSON
   654→claude_extract_json() {
   655→    local output="$1"
   656→
   657→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
   658→    local extracted
   659→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")
   660→
   661→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
   662→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
   663→        echo "$extracted"
   664→        return 0
   665→    fi
   666→
   667→    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
   668→    # Match from first { to last }
   669→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
   670→        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
   671→    else
   672→        echo "$output"
   673→    fi
   674→}
   675→
   676→# ============================================================================
   677→# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
   678→# ============================================================================
   679→
   680→# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
   681→# Args:
   682→#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
   683→# Returns:
   684→#   Path to workspace directory
   685→workspace_create() {
   686→    local identifier="$1"
   687→    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"
   688→
   689→    # Create workspace directory
   690→    mkdir -p "$workspace"
   691→
   692→    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
   693→    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
   694→{
   695→  "hooks": {},
   696→  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
   697→}
   698→EOF
   699→
   700→    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
   701→    echo "$workspace"
   702→}
   703→
   704→# Cleanup workspace
   705→# Args:
   706→#   $1 - workspace path
   707→workspace_cleanup() {
   708→    local workspace="$1"
   709→
   710→    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
   711→        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
   712→        rm -rf "$workspace"
   713→    fi
   714→}
   715→
   716→# ============================================================================
   717→# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
   718→# ============================================================================
   719→
   720→# Validate that value is non-negative integer
   721→# Args:
   722→#   $1 - value to validate
   723→# Returns:
   724→#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
   725→util_validate_count() {
   726→    local value="$1"
   727→
   728→    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
   729→        return 0
   730→    else
   731→        return 1
   732→    fi
   733→}
   734→
   735→# Get file size (cross-platform)
   736→# Args:
   737→#   $1 - file path
   738→# Returns:
   739→#   File size in bytes
   740→util_get_file_size() {
   741→    local file="$1"
   742→
   743→    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
   744→        echo "0"
   745→        return 0
   746→    fi
   747→
   748→    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
   749→    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
   750→        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
   751→    else
   752→        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
   753→        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
   754→    fi
   755→}
   756→
   757→# Create session directory if missing
   758→# Args:
   759→#   $1 - session_id
   760→util_create_session_dir() {
   761→    local session_id="$1"
   762→    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
   763→}
   764→
   765→# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
   766→# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
   767→# Args:
   768→#   $1 - transcript file path
   769→# Returns:
   770→#   Estimated token count
   771→util_calculate_tokens() {
   772→    local transcript="$1"
   773→
   774→    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
   775→        echo "0"
   776→        return 0
   777→    fi
   778→
   779→    # Count characters and divide by 4
   780→    local chars
   781→    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   782→    echo $((chars / 4))
   783→}
   784→

</system-reminder>

#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize session-specific logging
# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Globals:
#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
log_init() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Log initialization
    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH
    if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        command -v claude
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"

    # Invoke Claude with timeout
    local output
    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        --workspace "$workspace" \
        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory
    mkdir -p "$workspace"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

#!/bin/bash
# test-logging.sh - Unit tests for logging functions
#
# Tests the LOGGING namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    # Create temp directory for test session
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
    TEST_SESSION_ID="test-$(date +%s)-$$"

    # Source common.sh
    # Temporarily disable error trap to set up our test environment
    set +e
    trap - ERR

    # We need to mock path_get_session_dir before sourcing common.sh
    # Create a minimal implementation
    export SIDEKICK_TEST_MODE=1
    export SIDEKICK_ROOT="${TEST_DIR}"

    # Source the library
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true

    # Re-enable strict mode
    set -euo pipefail

    # Override path functions for testing
    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "${TEST_DIR}"
    }

    path_get_session_dir() {
        local session_id="$1"
        local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
        echo "$session_dir"
    }
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper: run a test and track results
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test: log_init creates log file
test_log_init_creates_file() {
    local session_id="test-init-$$"
    log_init "$session_id"

    local expected_log="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log"
    [ -f "$expected_log" ]
}

# Test: log_init writes initialization message
test_log_init_writes_message() {
    local session_id="test-init-msg-$$"
    log_init "$session_id"

    local log_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log"
    grep -q "Sidekick session started: ${session_id}" "$log_file"
}

# Test: log_debug respects LOG_LEVEL
test_log_debug_respects_level() {
    local session_id="test-debug-level-$$"
    log_init "$session_id"

    # Test with LOG_LEVEL=info (debug should not log)
    LOG_LEVEL=info
    log_debug "debug message should not appear"

    local log_file="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log"
    ! grep -q "debug message should not appear" "$log_file"
}

# Test: log_debug outputs when LOG_LEVEL=debug
test_log_debug_outputs_when_enabled() {
    local session_id="test-debug-enabled-$$"
    log_init "$session_id"

    # Test with LOG_LEVEL=debug
    LOG_LEVEL=debug
    log_debug "debug message should appear"

#!/bin/bash
# test-logging.sh - Unit tests for logging functions
#
# Tests the LOGGING namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    # Create temp directory for test session
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
    TEST_SESSION_ID="test-$(date +%s)-$$"

    # Source common.sh
    # Temporarily disable error trap to set up our test environment
    set +e
    trap - ERR

    # We need to mock path_get_session_dir before sourcing common.sh
    # Create a minimal implementation
    export SIDEKICK_TEST_MODE=1
    export SIDEKICK_ROOT="${TEST_DIR}"

    # Source the library
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true

    # Re-enable strict mode
    set -euo pipefail

    # Override path functions for testing
    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "${TEST_DIR}"
    }

    path_get_session_dir() {
        local session_id="$1"
        local session_dir="${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
        echo "$session_dir"
    }
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper: run a test and track results
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

#!/bin/bash
# test-config.sh - Unit tests for configuration functions
#
# Tests the CONFIGURATION namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
    TEST_SIDEKICK_ROOT="${TEST_DIR}/sidekick"
    mkdir -p "${TEST_SIDEKICK_ROOT}/lib"

    # Create minimal config.defaults
    cat > "${TEST_SIDEKICK_ROOT}/config.defaults" <<'EOF'
# Test defaults
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
FEATURE_RESUME=true
FEATURE_TRACKING=false
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
LOG_LEVEL=info
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2
SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7
EOF

    # Mock path_get_sidekick_root
    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "${TEST_SIDEKICK_ROOT}"
    }
    export -f path_get_sidekick_root

    # Source common.sh
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    # Reset config before each test
    unset FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION FEATURE_RESUME FEATURE_TRACKING
    unset TOPIC_MODE TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH LOG_LEVEL
    unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test: config_load sources defaults
test_config_load_sources_defaults() {
    config_load

    # Check that defaults were loaded
    [ "${FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION}" = "true" ]
    [ "${TOPIC_MODE}" = "topic-only" ]
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "info" ]
}

# Test: config_load loads user config override
test_config_load_user_override() {
    # Create user config
    mkdir -p "${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    cat > "${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
# User overrides
TOPIC_MODE=incremental
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    config_load

    # User config should override defaults
    [ "${TOPIC_MODE}" = "incremental" ]
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "debug" ]
    # But unoverridden values should remain
    [ "${FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION}" = "true" ]

    # Cleanup
    rm -f "${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
}

# Test: config_load project config overrides user
test_config_load_project_override() {
    # Create user config
    mkdir -p "${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    cat > "${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
TOPIC_MODE=incremental
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF

    # Create project config
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${TEST_DIR}/project"
    mkdir -p "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    cat > "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" <<'EOF'
TOPIC_MODE=full-analytics
EOF

    config_load

    # Project should override user
    [ "${TOPIC_MODE}" = "full-analytics" ]
    # But user should override defaults
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "debug" ]

    # Cleanup
    rm -f "${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
}

# Test: config_get returns value
test_config_get_returns_value() {
    config_load

    local value
    value=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODE")
    [ "$value" = "topic-only" ]
}

# Test: config_get returns empty for missing key
test_config_get_missing_key() {
    config_load

    local value
    value=$(config_get "NONEXISTENT_KEY")
    [ -z "$value" ]
}

# Test: config_is_feature_enabled returns 0 for enabled
test_config_is_feature_enabled_true() {
    config_load

    # FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true in defaults
    config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"
}

# Test: config_is_feature_enabled returns 1 for disabled
test_config_is_feature_enabled_false() {
    config_load

    # FEATURE_TRACKING=false in defaults
    ! config_is_feature_enabled "tracking"
}

# Test: config_is_feature_enabled handles uppercase
test_config_is_feature_enabled_case_insensitive() {
    config_load

    # Should convert to uppercase
    config_is_feature_enabled "resume"
    config_is_feature_enabled "RESUME"
}

# Test: _config_validate catches invalid LOG_LEVEL
test_config_validate_invalid_log_level() {
    # Set invalid log level
    LOG_LEVEL="invalid"

    # Validation should warn and reset to info
    local output
    output=$(_config_validate 2>&1 || true)
    echo "$output" | grep -q "Invalid LOG_LEVEL"
}

# Test: _config_validate accepts valid LOG_LEVEL
test_config_validate_valid_log_level() {
    LOG_LEVEL="debug"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "debug" ]

    LOG_LEVEL="info"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "info" ]

    LOG_LEVEL="warn"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "warn" ]

    LOG_LEVEL="error"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "error" ]
}

# Test: _config_validate checks numeric values
test_config_validate_numeric_values() {
    TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
    SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2
    SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7

    _config_validate
}

# Test: _config_validate rejects non-numeric values
test_config_validate_rejects_non_numeric() {
    TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH="not a number"

    ! _config_validate 2>/dev/null
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running configuration namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_config_load_sources_defaults
    run_test test_config_load_user_override
    run_test test_config_load_project_override
    run_test test_config_get_returns_value
    run_test test_config_get_missing_key
    run_test test_config_is_feature_enabled_true
    run_test test_config_is_feature_enabled_false
    run_test test_config_is_feature_enabled_case_insensitive
    run_test test_config_validate_invalid_log_level
    run_test test_config_validate_valid_log_level
    run_test test_config_validate_numeric_values
    run_test test_config_validate_rejects_non_numeric

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

# Test: config_is_feature_enabled returns 0 for enabled
test_config_is_feature_enabled_true() {
    config_load

    # FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true in defaults
    config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"
}

# Test: config_is_feature_enabled returns 1 for disabled
test_config_is_feature_enabled_false() {
    config_load

    # FEATURE_TRACKING=false in defaults
    ! config_is_feature_enabled "tracking"
}

# Test: config_is_feature_enabled handles uppercase
test_config_is_feature_enabled_case_insensitive() {
    config_load

    # Should convert to uppercase
    config_is_feature_enabled "resume"
    config_is_feature_enabled "RESUME"
}

# Test: _config_validate catches invalid LOG_LEVEL
test_config_validate_invalid_log_level() {
    # Set invalid log level
    LOG_LEVEL="invalid"

    # Validation should warn and reset to info
    local output
    output=$(_config_validate 2>&1 || true)
    echo "$output" | grep -q "Invalid LOG_LEVEL"
}

# Test: _config_validate accepts valid LOG_LEVEL
test_config_validate_valid_log_level() {
    LOG_LEVEL="debug"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "debug" ]

    LOG_LEVEL="info"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "info" ]

    LOG_LEVEL="warn"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "warn" ]

    LOG_LEVEL="error"
    _config_validate
    [ "${LOG_LEVEL}" = "error" ]
}

# Test: _config_validate checks numeric values
test_config_validate_numeric_values() {
    TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
    SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2
    SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7

    _config_validate
}

# Test: _config_validate rejects non-numeric values
test_config_validate_rejects_non_numeric() {
    TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH="not a number"

    ! _config_validate 2>/dev/null
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running configuration namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_config_load_sources_defaults
    run_test test_config_load_user_override
    run_test test_config_load_project_override
    run_test test_config_get_returns_value
    run_test test_config_get_missing_key
    run_test test_config_is_feature_enabled_true
    run_test test_config_is_feature_enabled_false
    run_test test_config_is_feature_enabled_case_insensitive
    run_test test_config_validate_invalid_log_level
    run_test test_config_validate_valid_log_level
    run_test test_config_validate_numeric_values
    run_test test_config_validate_rejects_non_numeric

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
# test-paths.sh - Unit tests for path resolution functions
#
# Tests the PATH RESOLUTION namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    # Source common.sh (we'll override path functions as needed for tests)
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    # Reset state before each test
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=""
    unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test: path_detect_scope detects user scope
test_path_detect_scope_user() {
    # Mock BASH_SOURCE to simulate running from ~/.claude
    BASH_SOURCE[1]="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"

    local scope
    scope=$(path_detect_scope)
    [ "$scope" = "user" ]
}

# Test: path_detect_scope detects project scope
test_path_detect_scope_project() {
    # Mock BASH_SOURCE to simulate running from project .claude
    BASH_SOURCE[1]="/some/project/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"

    local scope
    scope=$(path_detect_scope)
    [ "$scope" = "project" ]
}

# Test: path_get_sidekick_root returns correct path
test_path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # This will return the actual sidekick root based on common.sh location
    local root
    root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Should end with src/sidekick (since common.sh is in src/sidekick/lib)
    [[ "$root" == */src/sidekick ]]
}

# Test: path_get_sidekick_root caches result
test_path_get_sidekick_root_caches() {
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

    # First call
    local root1
    root1=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Second call should return same value
    local root2
    root2=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    [ "$root1" = "$root2" ]
}

# Test: path_get_session_dir creates directory
test_path_get_session_dir_creates() {
    # Override path_get_sidekick_root for this test
    local original_sidekick_root
    original_sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "${TEST_DIR}"
    }

    local session_id="test-session-123"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    # Should create the directory
    [ -d "$session_dir" ]

    # Should have correct path
    [ "$session_dir" = "${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}" ]

    # Restore
    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "$original_sidekick_root"
    }
}

# Test: path_get_session_dir returns existing directory
test_path_get_session_dir_existing() {
    path_get_sidekick_root() {
        echo "${TEST_DIR}"
    }

    local session_id="test-existing-123"
    mkdir -p "${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    [ "$session_dir" = "${TEST_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}" ]
}

# Test: path_get_project_dir from JSON
test_path_get_project_dir_from_json() {
    local json='{"workspace":{"project_dir":"/test/project"}}'

    local project_dir
    project_dir=$(path_get_project_dir "$json")

    [ "$project_dir" = "/test/project" ]
}

# Test: path_get_project_dir from environment
test_path_get_project_dir_from_env() {
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="/env/project"

    local project_dir
    project_dir=$(path_get_project_dir "")

    [ "$project_dir" = "/env/project" ]
}

# Test: path_get_project_dir falls back to pwd
test_path_get_project_dir_fallback_pwd() {
    unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR

    local project_dir
    project_dir=$(path_get_project_dir "")

    # Should return current directory
    [ -n "$project_dir" ]
    [ -d "$project_dir" ]
}

# Test: _path_normalize removes trailing slash
test_path_normalize_trailing_slash() {
    local normalized
    normalized=$(_path_normalize "/test/path/")

    [ "$normalized" = "/test/path" ]
}

# Test: _path_normalize handles existing paths
test_path_normalize_existing_path() {
    # Use a path we know exists
    local normalized
    normalized=$(_path_normalize "/tmp")

    # Should be absolute
    [[ "$normalized" == /* ]]
}

# Test: _path_normalize handles non-existing paths
test_path_normalize_nonexisting_path() {
    local normalized
    normalized=$(_path_normalize "/nonexistent/path/")

    # Should remove trailing slash
    [ "$normalized" = "/nonexistent/path" ]
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running path resolution namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_path_detect_scope_user
    run_test test_path_detect_scope_project
    run_test test_path_get_sidekick_root
    run_test test_path_get_sidekick_root_caches
    run_test test_path_get_session_dir_creates
    run_test test_path_get_session_dir_existing
    run_test test_path_get_project_dir_from_json
    run_test test_path_get_project_dir_from_env
    run_test test_path_get_project_dir_fallback_pwd
    run_test test_path_normalize_trailing_slash
    run_test test_path_normalize_existing_path
    run_test test_path_normalize_nonexisting_path

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
# test-json.sh - Unit tests for JSON processing functions
#
# Tests the JSON PROCESSING namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    # Source common.sh
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test: json_get extracts simple value
test_json_get_simple_value() {
    local json='{"name":"test","value":123}'

    local name
    name=$(json_get "$json" ".name")
    [ "$name" = "test" ]

    local value
    value=$(json_get "$json" ".value")
    [ "$value" = "123" ]
}

# Test: json_get extracts nested value
test_json_get_nested_value() {
    local json='{"workspace":{"project_dir":"/test/path"}}'

    local project_dir
    project_dir=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir")
    [ "$project_dir" = "/test/path" ]
}

# Test: json_get returns empty for missing key
test_json_get_missing_key() {
    local json='{"name":"test"}'

    local value
    value=$(json_get "$json" ".nonexistent")
    [ -z "$value" ]
}

# Test: json_get handles null values
test_json_get_null_value() {
    local json='{"name":null}'

    local value
    value=$(json_get "$json" ".name")
    [ -z "$value" ]
}

# Test: json_get_session_id extracts session_id
test_json_get_session_id() {
    local json='{"session_id":"abc-123-def"}'

    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$json")
    [ "$session_id" = "abc-123-def" ]
}

# Test: json_get_transcript_path extracts transcript_path
test_json_get_transcript_path() {
    local json='{"transcript_path":"/path/to/transcript.jsonl"}'

    local transcript_path
    transcript_path=$(json_get_transcript_path "$json")
    [ "$transcript_path" = "/path/to/transcript.jsonl" ]
}

# Test: json_validate accepts valid JSON
test_json_validate_valid() {
    local json='{"name":"test","value":123}'

    json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_validate rejects invalid JSON
test_json_validate_invalid() {
    local json='{"name":"test",invalid}'

    ! json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_validate accepts array
test_json_validate_array() {
    local json='[1,2,3]'

    json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_validate accepts empty object
test_json_validate_empty_object() {
    local json='{}'

    json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_extract_from_markdown extracts from code block
test_json_extract_from_markdown_code_block() {
    local text='```json
{"name":"test"}
```'

    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$text")
    [ "$extracted" = '{"name":"test"}' ]
}

# Test: json_extract_from_markdown returns as-is if not wrapped
test_json_extract_from_markdown_unwrapped() {
    local text='{"name":"test"}'

    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$text")
    [ "$extracted" = '{"name":"test"}' ]
}

# Test: json_extract_from_markdown handles multiline JSON
test_json_extract_from_markdown_multiline() {
    local text='```json
{
  "name": "test",
  "value": 123
}
```'

    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$text")

    # Should have extracted the JSON
    json_validate "$extracted"

    local name
    name=$(json_get "$extracted" ".name")
    [ "$name" = "test" ]
}

# Test: json_get handles special characters
test_json_get_special_characters() {
    local json='{"message":"Hello \"World\""}'

    local message
    message=$(json_get "$json" ".message")
    [ "$message" = 'Hello "World"' ]
}

# Test: json_get handles unicode
test_json_get_unicode() {
    local json='{"emoji":"🚀"}'

    local emoji
    emoji=$(json_get "$json" ".emoji")
    [ "$emoji" = "🚀" ]
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running JSON processing namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_json_get_simple_value
    run_test test_json_get_nested_value
    run_test test_json_get_missing_key
    run_test test_json_get_null_value
    run_test test_json_get_session_id
    run_test test_json_get_transcript_path
    run_test test_json_validate_valid
    run_test test_json_validate_invalid
    run_test test_json_validate_array
    run_test test_json_validate_empty_object
    run_test test_json_extract_from_markdown_code_block
    run_test test_json_extract_from_markdown_unwrapped
    run_test test_json_extract_from_markdown_multiline
    run_test test_json_get_special_characters
    run_test test_json_get_unicode

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
# test-json.sh - Unit tests for JSON processing functions
#
# Tests the JSON PROCESSING namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    # Source common.sh
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test: json_get extracts simple value
test_json_get_simple_value() {
    local json='{"name":"test","value":123}'

    local name
    name=$(json_get "$json" ".name")
    [ "$name" = "test" ]

    local value
    value=$(json_get "$json" ".value")
    [ "$value" = "123" ]
}

# Test: json_get extracts nested value
test_json_get_nested_value() {
    local json='{"workspace":{"project_dir":"/test/path"}}'

    local project_dir
    project_dir=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir")
    [ "$project_dir" = "/test/path" ]
}

# Test: json_get returns empty for missing key
test_json_get_missing_key() {
    local json='{"name":"test"}'

    local value
    value=$(json_get "$json" ".nonexistent")
    [ -z "$value" ]
}

# Test: json_get handles null values
test_json_get_null_value() {
    local json='{"name":null}'

    local value
    value=$(json_get "$json" ".name")
    [ -z "$value" ]
}

# Test: json_get_session_id extracts session_id
test_json_get_session_id() {
    local json='{"session_id":"abc-123-def"}'

    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$json")
    [ "$session_id" = "abc-123-def" ]
}

# Test: json_get_transcript_path extracts transcript_path
test_json_get_transcript_path() {
    local json='{"transcript_path":"/path/to/transcript.jsonl"}'

    local transcript_path
    transcript_path=$(json_get_transcript_path "$json")
    [ "$transcript_path" = "/path/to/transcript.jsonl" ]
}

# Test: json_validate accepts valid JSON
test_json_validate_valid() {
    local json='{"name":"test","value":123}'

    json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_validate rejects invalid JSON
test_json_validate_invalid() {
    local json='{"name":"test",invalid}'

    ! json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_validate accepts array
test_json_validate_array() {
    local json='[1,2,3]'

    json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_validate accepts empty object
test_json_validate_empty_object() {
    local json='{}'

    json_validate "$json"
}

# Test: json_extract_from_markdown extracts from code block
test_json_extract_from_markdown_code_block() {
    local text='```json
{"name":"test"}
```'

    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$text")
    [ "$extracted" = '{"name":"test"}' ]
}

# Test: json_extract_from_markdown returns as-is if not wrapped
test_json_extract_from_markdown_unwrapped() {
    local text='{"name":"test"}'

    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$text")
    [ "$extracted" = '{"name":"test"}' ]
}

# Test: json_extract_from_markdown handles multiline JSON
test_json_extract_from_markdown_multiline() {
    local text='```json
{
  "name": "test",
  "value": 123
}
```'

    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$text")

    # Should have extracted the JSON
    json_validate "$extracted"

    local name
    name=$(json_get "$extracted" ".name")
    [ "$name" = "test" ]
}

# Test: json_get handles special characters
test_json_get_special_characters() {
    local json='{"message":"Hello \"World\""}'

    local message
    message=$(json_get "$json" ".message")
    [ "$message" = 'Hello "World"' ]
}

# Test: json_get handles unicode
test_json_get_unicode() {
    local json='{"emoji":"🚀"}'

    local emoji
    emoji=$(json_get "$json" ".emoji")
    [ "$emoji" = "🚀" ]
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running JSON processing namespace tests..."
    echo

    setup

    # Run all tests
    run_test test_json_get_simple_value
    run_test test_json_get_nested_value
    run_test test_json_get_missing_key
    run_test test_json_get_null_value
    run_test test_json_get_session_id
    run_test test_json_get_transcript_path
    run_test test_json_validate_valid
    run_test test_json_validate_invalid
    run_test test_json_validate_array
    run_test test_json_validate_empty_object
    run_test test_json_extract_from_markdown_code_block
    run_test test_json_extract_from_markdown_unwrapped
    run_test test_json_extract_from_markdown_multiline
    run_test test_json_get_special_characters
    run_test test_json_get_unicode

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"
